headline: Matriculation vs STPM Debate Resurfaces After 2026 Intake
context: The 2026 matriculation intake of 40,000 students reignited debate over dual-track university entry fairness.

cards.big: 90% matriculation quota for Bumiputera students remains since 2003
cards.sub: Non-Bumiputera students compete for 4,000 remaining spots

cards.big: STPM pass rate is 62% versus matriculation pass rate of 95% in 2025
cards.sub: Critics argue the two pathways are not academically equivalent

cards.big: Article 153 provides for reasonable Bumiputera quotas in education
cards.sub: Constitutional provision does not specify exact percentage thresholds

cards.big: World Bank 2024 report noted dual-track systems reduce meritocratic signaling
cards.sub: Employers increasingly discount pre-university qualifications in hiring

cards.big: A single rigorous pre-university standard would benefit all communities equally
Can affirmative action coexist with a unified pre-university examination
